Setting Up Python 3 venv in VSCode
Working with Python 3 and needed a virtual environment. I use VSCode, so here's how to configure VSCode's Python 3 interpreter.
Set up a test project. In a terminal:
language-cmd shiki shiki-themes github-light github-light github-dark-high-contrast

mkdir pyTest # create folder
cd pyTest # cd in
Open it in VSCode. If you've installed the VSCode quick-launcher, just

python3 -m venv .venv # Mac
python -m venv .venv # Windows
Per VSCode's official docs, you should see this prompt:
(Translation: "We detected a new virtual environment. Use it for the current workspace?")
Click Yes and you're done. But for some reason my VSCode just... doesn't show this prompt.
To work around it, do it manually. Open the Command Palette:
VSCode's bottom-left now shows the current Python interpreter.
I'd recommend restarting VSCode so it picks up the change. Normally you'll see VSCode auto-run:
That means you're now using the
A clean environment — install whatever packages you need.
doesn't show up, fully quit and restart VSCode.
